question:
The image below shows greener take-away food containers and cutlery. It consists of
wax-lined cardboard boxes and cutlery made from compostable materials.

(a)

(i) Explain how the compostable cutlery can be considered greener.             [2]
answer:
Explain how the ‘compostable’ cutlery can be considered
             ‘greener’.
             Answers relating to the compostable cutlery being greener should be awarded
             up to 2 marks.

             Guidance:
             Candidates must be able to explain the environmental benefits of the cutlery
             being compostable. This negates the usual disposal of plastic take away cutlery,
             compostable means a much more-friendly product, that can break down into
             non-toxic components. Reduce landfill Compostable does not mean
             biodegradable.

             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                           0
             Simplistic or basic response

             They are more environmentally friendly than plastic cutlery
             More detailed response
             Compostable cutlery are much more environmentally friendly and will break

             down into non-toxic components.

question:
The image below shows greener take-away food containers and cutlery. It consists of
wax-lined cardboard boxes and cutlery made from compostable materials.

(a)

(ii) The food containers are made from cardboard which has a waxed coating applied
to the inside. Describe the reason for the waxed coating.                  [3]
answer:
The food containers are made from cardboard which has a
             waxed coating applied to the inside. Describe the reason for
             the waxed coating.
             Answers relating to the reason for wax coating should be awarded up to
             3 marks.

             Guidance:
             Candidates need to provide reasons for the wax lining / coating – this is to
             waterproof the cardboard, to let it resist moisture from food products / possible
             sauces / vinegar etc, it prevents the takeaway container from becoming soggy
             or perforating, the food could spill out. The wax allows foods / meats to be
             stored appropriately, transported rigidly, and even frozen inside the containers.
             The wax coating offers some thermal properties. Insulating the food and
             keeping it warmer for longer and making the container easier to handle, The
             wax coating also has some non-stick properties stopping the food stick to the
             box. DO NOT credit statements that refer to recyclability – the wax lining
             prevents recycling. Wax lined cardboard boxes are still biodegradable however.

             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                           0
             Simplistic or basic response

             The wax makes the cardboard suitable for food
             More detailed response

             The wax makes the cardboard more rigid and resistant to liquids / moisture.
             A fully detailed response
             The wax coating increases rigidity and prevents contents from weakening or

             softening the container, allowing food to be transported, stored or carried safely.

question:
The image below shows greener take-away food containers and cutlery. It consists of
wax-lined cardboard boxes and cutlery made from compostable materials.

(b)

(i) The food containers have been mass produced using die cutting. Explain in detail
how the die cutting process is suitable for producing the food containers.   [4]
answer:
The food containers have been mass produced using die-
             cutting. Explain in detail how the die-cutting process is
             suitable for producing the food container.
             Answers relating to explaining how die cutting is suitable for the food container
             should be awarded up to 4 marks.

             Guidance:
             Candidates need to identify that die cutting allows the outline to be cut from
             sheet cardboard repetitively, and identically for mass production. The outer
             profile of the die features sharp edges to cut. The die cutting process also uses
             rounded blades to crease the internal features of the food container for folding.
             Shapes can be tessellated / arranged to reduce waste. Cuts and scores
             complex shapes easily. Reduces waste of card therefore costs of materials.
             Product can be stored easily afterwards as it stays flat. Quick process for mass
             production. DO NOT credit repetitive statements. Responses for 4 marks must
             include both cutting / outline and folding / creasing information.
             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                         0
             Simplistic or basic response
             Allows lots of containers to be made

             Or
             Cuts the same shape repeatedly
             More detailed response
             Allows the same shape to be cut quickly and efficiently
             Or                                                                                   2
             The food container is quickly cut out of the sheet material in a quick and
             accurate process.
             Detailed explanation or response covering cut lines and fold lines
             Die cutting allows the shape to be cut from sheet material and also how the          3
             container folds together.
             Highly detailed response with explanation of both cut lines and fold lines
             Die cutting is a quick, efficient and suitable industrial process for making large
             numbers of containers. The die is designed to cut out the outline and the slot,      4
             and also crease the fold lines so the box can be easily constructed, repeatedly
             and identically so all boxes are the same.

question:
The image below shows greener take-away food containers and cutlery. It consists of
wax-lined cardboard boxes and cutlery made from compostable materials.

(b)

(ii) Describe the reason for the packaging feature shown below, labelled A.          [2]

A
answer:
Describe the reason for the packaging feature shown below
             labelled A.
             Answers relating to feature A should be awarded up to 2 marks.

             Guidance:
             The responses gaining credit must describe the reason for feature A, naming it
             as a locking tab is a low-level response. This locking tab labelled feature A will
             fit into a corresponding slot when the packaging is assembled and the notch
             helps ‘lock’ it closed.
             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                         0
             Simplistic or basic response
             Feature A helps the box to stay closed                                               1
             Or
             Feature A is a locking tab                                                           1
             More detailed response
             This is a locking tab, which helps keep the box closed when the shape fits into      2
             the slot on another part of the food container,
             Or
             This locking tab closes the package keeping the contents insulated / protected       2
             inside.

question:
The image below shows greener take-away food containers and cutlery. It consists of
wax-lined cardboard boxes and cutlery made from compostable materials.

(c) The stainless steel drinking straw shown below is extendable and fits into a casing.

Explain how the stainless steel drinking straw provides a greener solution to a
worldwide problem.                                                                             [4]
answer:
Explain how the stainless steel drinking straw provides a
             ‘greener’ solution to a world-wide problem.
             Answers relating to how the stainless steel straw provides a solution to world
             wide problems should be awarded up to 4 marks.

             Guidance:
             Candidates should identify that throw away plastic straws create huge
             problems, in waste generation and also ocean eco systems. The stainless steel
             straw is retractable and fully reusable, built to be reused and long-lasting,
             portable and prevents a one-use plastic straw being issues by a vendor.

             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                         0
             Simplistic or basic response

             The stainless steel straw replaces plastic disposable ones.
             More detailed response
             The stainless steel straw replaces plastic disposable ones, which are thrown         2
             away in large numbers creating issues in oceans.
             A detailed response
             The stainless steel straw replaces plastic disposable ones, which are thrown
             away in large numbers creating issues in oceans. The stainless steel straw is        3
             reusable, and portable, and the user is providing their own straw, cutting down
             on the need for new straws and waste.
             A broad and fully detailed response
             The stainless steel straw replaces plastic disposable ones, which are thrown
             away in large numbers creating damage to ocean wildlife and eco systems and
             increasing landfill. The stainless steel straw is reusable, and portable and         4
             hygienic in its own carry case, and the user is providing their own straw, cutting
             down on the need for new straws and waste.

                                                                                      Total       15

question:
The two chairs shown below have been produced using Life Cycle Analysis as a design
strategy.

Chair A                                          Chair B
Made from used bicycle wheels and tyres           Made from worn car tyres and seat belts
Retail price £189                                Retail price £229

(a) Chair A has been designed for teenage cyclists. Explain how Chair A will appeal to this
target market.                                                                        [2]
answer:
Chair A has been designed for teenage cyclists. Explain
             how Chair A will appeal to this target market.
             Answers relating to a typical target market should be awarded up to 2 marks.

             Guidance:
             The design of Chair A must be considered, and how features fit the target
             market of teenagers. Responses may include:
             • Bright vibrant colour scheme suitable for study, bedroom, or games room.
             • The seating position looks quite reclined, suitable for teenagers, reading,
                relaxing, gaming, online, ergonomics not so appropriate for older users.
             • The cycling theme / use of used wheels – many teenagers like cycling, bmx
                riding, mountain biking, cycling sports so this fits well with their hobbies and
                interests.
             • The low footprint of chair A reusing wheels, reducing waste could be
                considered cool or desirable and appeal to teenage users.
             • Consumers including teens are now more environmentally aware and are
                picking products that have environmental considerations.
             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                           0
             Simplistic or basic response
             Chair A will appeal to teenagers because of the used cycle wheels in the               1
             structure
             Or
             Chair A will appeal to teenagers because of the bright colours                         1
             More detailed response
             Chair A is intended to suit sporty teenagers who like reading or relaxing in a

             comfortable reclined position. The chair is bright, sporty and would fit well in a
             bedroom, study or games / sports room.

question:
The two chairs shown below have been produced using Life Cycle Analysis as a design
strategy.

Chair A                                          Chair B
Made from used bicycle wheels and tyres           Made from worn car tyres and seat belts
Retail price £189                                Retail price £229

(b) Explain how Chair B provides an economic benefit to the manufacturer.                  [3]
answer:
Describe how Chair B provides an economical benefit to
             the manufacturer.
             Answers relating to economic benefit to the manufacturer should be awarded up
             to 3 marks.
             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                           0
             Simplistic or basic response
             The chair is made using used materials which saves buying new material.                1
             Or
             The used tyres and seat belts will not be costly to source and retails at £229         1
             More detailed response
             The tyres and seatbelts used are the main materials and will be cheap to source        2
             and very little costs to reprocess,
             Or
             Selling at £229 should provide a high profit margin as there are low materials         2
             sourcing costs
             A detailed response
             The tyres and seatbelts are easy to source and are available in large quantities,      3
             they are also used as the main materials and will be cheap to source and very
             little costs to reprocess, Selling at £229 should provide a high profit margin.
             Or
             There will always be a evolving target market who will be prepared to purchase         3
             this type of Chair – games room, study, bar etc. This will provide a constant
             need and the supply will secure a consistent profit.

question:
The two chairs shown below have been produced using Life Cycle Analysis as a design
strategy.

Chair A                                          Chair B
Made from used bicycle wheels and tyres           Made from worn car tyres and seat belts
Retail price £189                                Retail price £229

(c) Describe how the Life Cycle Analysis strategy has been used to design and develop
Chair A and Chair B.                                                              [5]
answer:
Describe how the Life Cycle Analysis strategy has been
             used to design and develop Chair A and B.
             Answers relating to how the LCA strategy has been used to produce the chairs
             should be awarded up to 5 marks.

             Guidance:
             • Life cycle analysis using ‘cradle to cradle’ principles
             • Tyres / seat belts at end of their useful life being re-used
             • Saving sourcing / processing new materials
             • Improving disposal of waste products
             • No recycling required / no reprocessing needed.
             • Avoiding ‘cradle to grave’ concept
             • More sustainable product development
             • Lower environmental footprint
             • Creative / innovative solution

             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed
             Brief description, very little illustration of LCA, simplistic or basic response
             The chairs have been made from used / waste bicycle parts, wheels and tyres          1-2
             which saves finding new materials.
             More detailed description showing more understanding of LCA AND how this
             has been used to produce the chair
             As a result of LCA, when bicycles, or parts like wheels, tyres, and seatbelts
             come to the end of their useful life, they can be reused / recycled and used in      3-4
             another product. In this case, they are bonded together to create the chair frame
             / structure. This reduces waste and makes good use of previously used
             materials.
             A clear and detailed description of LCA AND how this has been used to create
             the chairs.
             As a result of LCA, when bicycles, or parts like tyres and wheels, come to the
             end of their useful life on the bicycle, they can be reused / recycled and used in
             another product. In this case, there is very little reprocessing of the reclaimed

             wheels, they are bonded together to create the chair frame / structure. This
             reduces waste and makes good use of previously used materials, producing a
             very small environmental footprint for the chairs, using a ‘cradle to cradle’
             approach.

                                                                                    Total         10

question:
The space-saving clothes dryer shown below can be wall mounted indoors or outdoors.

Product information:

•               Mild steel body that opens and closes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              magnet
•               6 × aluminium clothes bars.
•               Steel wings that keep aluminium bars
parallel.
•               Stainless steel M5 bolts, washers and
locking nuts used as a hinge.
•               Product weight 4 kg.
•               Safety tested to hang up to 3 kg of
clothing per bar.
•               Magnet fitted to the inside of the back of
the clothes dryer.

(a)

(i) Give two reasons why mild steel is a suitable material for the body of the clothes
dryer.                                                                       2 × [2]

Reason 1: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

Reason 2: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

(ii)              Explain why a magnet is fitted to the back of the clothes dryer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           [2]

(iii)               The mild steel body parts have a paint finish applied. Describe two advantages
this gives the clothes dryer.                                              2 × [2]

Advantage 1: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

Advantage 2: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
answer:
Give two reasons why mild steel is a suitable material for the
                                                                                               ✓      2x [2]
               body of the clothes dryer.
               Answers that describe two reasons for the suitability of mild steel can be
               awarded up to 2 x 2 marks

               Guidance:
               The main benefits of using mild steel include structural strength, rigidity, ability
               to support the loaded weight of the bars, ability to be coated to reduce
               corrosion. Mild steel is ideal for the body of the clothes dryer as it can be
               deformed to the shapes required. Responses relating to colour and finish may
               also be acceptable. DO NOT credit repetitive responses.

               No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                             0
               Simplistic or basic response
               Mild steel is a strong and durable material                                              1
               Or
               Mild steel is a rigid material to support the weight.                                    1
               Or
               Mild steel can be easily shaped for the clothes dryer parts                              1
               Or
               Mild steel can be pressed and retain the shape                                           1
               More detailed response
               Mild steel is a strong and durable material that is easily deformed to the shape         2
               required which will withstand the forces of a fully loaded dryer without
               compromise.
               Or
               Mild steel provides a rigid structure when deformed that will be corrosion               2
               resistant when finished with the coating as shown, providing a reliable and
               long-lasting product.

question:
The space-saving clothes dryer shown below can be wall mounted indoors or outdoors.

Product information:

•               Mild steel body that opens and closes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              magnet
•               6 × aluminium clothes bars.
•               Steel wings that keep aluminium bars
parallel.
•               Stainless steel M5 bolts, washers and
locking nuts used as a hinge.
•               Product weight 4 kg.
•               Safety tested to hang up to 3 kg of
clothing per bar.
•               Magnet fitted to the inside of the back of
the clothes dryer.

(b) The image below shows the steel wings that hold each aluminium bar with a ‘U’-shaped
feature.

‘U’-shaped
feature

Enlarged view of ‘U’-shaped feature

(i)              Explain why the ‘U’-shaped feature is necessary, and what would happen if this
was not present.                                                               [4]

(ii)
During manufacture, the mild steel parts have been pressed from a sheet and
deformed using a bending jig.
Explain two benefits of using a bending jig during the manufacturing process.
2 × [2]

Benefit 1: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

Benefit 2: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
answer:
Explain why the ‘U’ shape feature is necessary, and what
               would happen if this was not present
               Answers relating to the reason for the U shape feature, and what would
               happen if this was not present should be awarded up to 4 marks. DO NOT
               credit repetitive responses

               Guidance:
               The U-shaped feature in needed to allow the sides to rotate the 90 degrees
               when opening and closing, despite the aluminium bars. The U shape allows
               the bars some ‘movement’ otherwise the aluminium bars would ‘jam’ as the
               sides open and close. The U shape caters for the diameter of the rods and the
               amount of movement required, and also stops the bars from going too far
               backwards or forwards, locking them into place.

               No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                    0
               Simplistic or basic response
               To keep the bars in the right place                                             1
               Or
               Stop the bars falling out                                                       1
               More detailed response about the U-shaped feature OR what would happen
               To allow the steel wings to pass over the aluminium bars when opening or        2
               closing.
               A detailed explanation about the U-shaped feature and what would happen
               This U-shaped feature allows the bars to be retained and the steel wings to

               fold inwards or outwards with the rigid aluminium bars in place, without the
               aluminium bars becoming ‘stuck’.
               A highly detailed response which fully explains the need for U feature and
               what would happen if it was not present.
               This U-shaped feature allows the bars to be fixed in the correct position and
               the steel wings to fold inwards or outwards with the rigid aluminium bars in

               place, without the aluminium bars becoming ‘stuck’. Without the U-shaped
               feature, the aluminium bars would become jammed and the dryer would not
               be able to close.

Explain two benefits of using a bending jig during the
                                                                                               ✓    2 x [2]
               manufacturing process.
               Answers explaining two different benefits of using a bending jig should be
               awarded up to 2 x [2] marks.

               Guidance:
               DO NOT credit repetitive responses. The bending jig will increase efficiency
               and accuracy / reduce errors or mistakes, the wings will all be identical (left
               and right), non-skilled workers may be used (reducing costs), scale of
               production can be increased, replacement parts can be readily available, large
               numbers can be produced quickly, quality control / quality assurance is easier
               to secure, less waste as defects are minimised.

               No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                           0
               Simplistic or basic response
               A bending jig will control the shape better                                            1
               Or
               Using a jig will make the process easier or quicker (than by hand)                     1
               More detailed explanation or response
               The bending jigs will increase the speed at which the parts can be made and            2
               ensure that they are the correct shape every time.
               Or
               Using the bending jig will ensure repeatability and provide increased quality          2
               control / assurance as there will be no decisions made by workers / operators.

(iii) The website where bottles can be purchased includes the ‘Officially Certified’

answer:
The website where bottles can be purchased includes the
               ‘Officially Certified’ logo. Explain how this may influence
               potential consumers.
               Answers relating to the confidence offered to customers by the ‘officially
               certified’ logo should be awarded up to 3 marks.

               No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                    0
               One simplistic or basic response
               The logo means the bottles have been tested                                     1
               Or
               The bottles are safe to use                                                     1
               More detailed explanation or response

               The bottles have been tested and are fit for purpose
               A fully detailed explanation
               The customer can be confident that the bottles are safe and fit for purpose
               because they have been repeatedly tested and deemed fit for intended use
               and therefore approved by a recognised body.

               Credit responses that refer to building the consumer’s ‘trust’ in the product
               when they see the logo displayed.

(b) During the development stages of the water bottle, a number of rapid prototypes were
produced for the sports cap, two of which are shown below.

(i) Analyse why the design team would produce a range of different sports caps
during the development stages.                                               [5]
answer:
Analyse why the design team would produce a range of
               different sports caps during the development stages.
               Answers analysing why the design teams would produce a range of sports
               caps during the development stages should be awarded up to 5 marks.

               Guidance:
               • During development, the ideas would need to be tested
               • There would be several ‘possible’ solutions and these would require
                  prototyping
               • The target market / users would be given the physical prototypes as
                  user trials, and feedback used to refine ideas
               • Physical outcomes would be produced to realise ideas
               • Function tests would be carried out to see how the different caps
                  worked
               • Results of testing would be analysed and lead to modifications and
                  further improvement.
               • Aspects that fail would be removed / altered to improve the design.
               • The iterative cycle requires testing of ideas in order for solution to be
                  fully evolved / developed.

               No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed                     0
               Brief description, very little analysis, simplistic or basic response
               The design team would want to test a physical outcome so would make a           1
               prototype to carry out testing.
               More detailed analysis showing more understanding of reasons why a
               range of sports caps would be produced during development.
               The design team would produce prototypes of ideas that they thought had         2-3
               potential. These would be tested, used in user trials, and the results
               analysed to improve any defects.
               A clear and detailed analysis of the reasons the design team would
               produce a range of different sports caps during the development stages.
               The design team would produce physical prototypes of ideas that they
               thought had potential. These would then be fully tested, used in user trials,
                                                                                               4-5
               and the results analysed to improve any defects. This would allow a
               chosen sports cap to be finalised and remove any defects prior to
               production.

question:
The water bottles shown below include a fruit infuser to allow users to flavour the water they
consume.

Product information:

•     650 ml capacity
•     Sports cap with spill-proof lid
•     Bottle is made from recycled polymer
•     Various colours available

Check out our 45 reviews

OFFICIALLY
CERTIFIED

All products are
100% officially
certified.

(b) During the development stages of the water bottle, a number of rapid prototypes were
produced for the sports cap, two of which are shown below.

(ii) An additive manufacturing process was used to create the sports caps shown.
Evaluate the benefits of additive manufacturing processes.                  [5]
answer:
Evaluate the benefits of additive manufacturing processes.
               Answers evaluating the impact additive manufacturing processes can have
               when rapid prototyping should be awarded up to 5 marks.

               Guidance:
               • Additive processes, like 3D printing, generate very little waste.
               • Lead in time is very short / reduced.
               • CAD files are converted quickly into CAM data / physical outcomes.
               • Very difficult shapes, like the sports cap, can be created quickly
               • Various sports caps can be produced in one-go / in one process for
                  testing or realising the designs
               • Additive machines are very versatile and can make different shapes
                  and forms
               • Colours can be changed easily and inexpensively
               • Additive machines can be left unattended / overnight for longer
                  processes
               • Following expensive purchase, prototypes are produced relatively
                  cheaply, which allows mistakes to be less costly than in full
                  manufacture
               • Ideas can be tested with the target market quickly, with regular reviews,
                  modifications and iterations

               No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed                   0
               Brief description, very little evaluation, simplistic or basic response
               The additive process allows an idea to be physically produced quickly for     1
               testing, and modifications can be made quickly and easily / cheaply.
               More detailed evaluation showing more understanding of impact of additive
               manufacturing processes when rapid prototyping.
               Additive processes produce very little waste and can create multiple
                                                                                             2-3
               identical or different complex shapes quickly and inexpensively. Additive
               processes can be easily adapted for different colours by changing the
               source material.
               A clear and detailed evaluation of impact of additive manufacturing
               processes when rapid prototyping.
               Additive manufacturing machines produce physical outcomes from CAD
               data quickly and efficiently. Initial costly investment of purchasing the
               machine is made, the production of items is very low. Additive processes      4-5
               produce very little waste and can create multiple identical or different
               complex shapes quickly and inexpensively. Additive processes can be
               easily adapted for different colours by changing the source material.

                                                                              Total          20

question:
The modern smart home device shown below was designed to reflect user-centred design.

(a) Analyse the meaning of user-centred design and how this has influenced the smart
home device.                                                                       [5]
answer:
Analyse the meaning of ‘User Centred Design’ and how this
             has influenced the ‘Smart Home’ device.
             Answers analysing the meaning of user centred design’ and how this has
             influenced the ‘Smart Home’ device should be awarded up to 5 marks.

             Guidance
             • User centred design is about designing a product to fit the needs and
                wants of the target market
             • The requirements will be established before designing
             • For the ‘Smart Home’ device, the target market will have been integral
                to the iterative design process
             • User feedback will be used throughout designing and development
             • The shape of the device – slimline, circular / cylindrical, white / grey
                and other aesthetics will be pre-determined
             • Functions like Wi-Fi connectivity, Bluetooth, tactile buttons
             • Powered by 240V mains power
             • Ease of connectivity to home Wi-Fi and other devices.

             Note: Detailed knowledge of ‘Alexa’ or ‘Echo Dot’ is not critical.

             No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed                       0
             Brief description, very little analysis, simplistic or basic response
             The smart home device has been designed with the user in mind to ensure
             that all of the requirements of the user are met by the final product. No         1
             reference to user centred design just a list of the products features with
             little explanation of their benefits to the user.
             More detailed analysis showing more understanding of ‘User centred
             design’ and this influencing the smart home device.
             Placing the user at the forefront, the device will be designed with the
             specific needs and wants of the target market as the driving forces. This
                                                                                              2-3
             will result in the device functioning and looking exactly how the user
             expects / requires, because the designers will ensure that the device
             matches all of the requirements set out. List of features with some
             qualification of how these help the user.
             A clear and detailed analysis of ‘User centred design’ and this influencing
             the smart home device.
             Placing the user at the forefront, the device will be designed with the
             specific needs and wants of the target market as the driving forces. This
             will result in the device meeting the expectations of the user in terms of
             quality, style, shape, function, materials, aesthetics, connectivity and         4-5
             compatibility with other devices. User centred design ensures that the
             target market is included at every point during the product’s development,
             so that feedback allows the product to be refined ensuring it’s eventual
             success.

             • The ease of managing devices without physically moving / operating
                them
             • The ability to add / remove additional devices makes the system very
                clever / useful
             • Provides opportunities to reduce costs / increase security at home
             • Gives home owner greater control of devices
             • Allows technology to provide a simpler / effective means of control

             (Note – not all of this indicative content is required to access 5 marks!)

             appropriate material with some coherence, some errors of grammar,

question:

types of furniture.

(i) The four sides of the tray are made from medium density fibreboard (MDF).
Describe two properties of MDF that make it suitable for the sides of the tray.
2 × [2]

Property 1: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

Property 2: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
answer:
Describe two properties of MDF that make it suitable for
                                                                                         ✓   2 x [2]
              the sides of the tray.
              Answers relating to the properties of MDF that make it suitable for the
              sides of the tray should be awarded up to 2 x [2] marks. Do not credit
              repetitive responses

              Guidance:
              • MDF is structurally suited to create the 4 sides for the tray
              • No grain for paint finish
              • Suitable for CNC machining (to create sides for tray)
              • Able to be glued / dowelled for secure and durable construction for
                 tray
              • MDF is a sustainable choice of material / reduces product footprint
              • Multiple products made from large (8x4) sheet
              • Rigid – strength/weight ratio
              • No grain – easy to paint finish
              • Able to be used with a range of fixtures and fittings
              • Do not credit responses that suggest ‘cheap’ we are looking for
                 physical properties.

              No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed                      0
              Simplistic or basic response
              MDF is a solid material so the tray will be sturdy                               1
              Or
              MDF is a manufactured board suitable for painting                                1
              More detailed explanation or response
              MDF is a sustainable and readily available material with no grain. It is         2
              rigid and has good strength-weight ratio and able to be cut / shaped /
              joined for the tray sides
              Or
              MDF is a manufactured board with no grain, suitable for taking a paint           2
              finish as required with the tray.

answer:
Explain the role of the spring fitted to the centre of the
              hinge.
              Answers describing the role of the spring should be awarded up to 3
              marks.

              No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                 0
              Simplistic or basic response

              The spring pushes the two sides of the hinge apart
              More detailed explanation or response
              The spring forces the opposite sides of the hinge apart keeping the tray     2
              flat
              A clear and detailed response
              The spring forces the opposite sides of the hinge apart keeping the tray
              flat, because the legs are kept under the tray. When hooked over the arm     3

question:
The portable tray below is designed for carrying refreshments and can securely fit onto most
types of furniture.

(b) The legs of the tray are hinged as shown below.

(iii) The hinges and screws are bought-in components. Explain how this benefits the
manufacturer when producing the tray using high volume production.           [4]
answer:
The hinges and screws are bought-in components. Explain
              how this benefits the manufacturer when producing the
              tray using high volume production.
              Answers explaining how bought-in hinges and screws benefits the
              manufacturer when producing the tray using high volume production
              should be awarded up to 4 marks.

              No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                     0
              Simplistic or basic response
              The manufacturer will only order the number of screws and hinges                 1
              needed
              More detailed explanation or response
              The manufacturer will only order and stock enough screws and hinges to           2
              produce the number of trays ordered / in demand.
              A detailed explanation or response
              The manufacturer will only order and stock enough screws and hinges to
              produce the number of trays ordered / in demand. This means it costs             3
              less, and there are no surplus items leftover, requiring additional storage
              space.
              A fully detailed and clear explanation or response
              The manufacturer will only order and stock enough screws and hinges to
              produce the number of trays ordered / in demand. This allows the
              company to divert investment to other areas of the company, distributing
              assets more evenly. Costs will be lower (JIT) There are no surplus items         4
              leftover, requiring additional storage space. It keeps the manufacturing
              process lean and efficient, and the bought-in items are quality assured
              allowing the manufacturer to focus on the manufacture of the other parts.

Explain the difference between the red and black lines on the CAD drawing when
manufacturing the tray handles using the CAM router.                                                                                                                [3]
answer:
Explain the difference between the red and black lines on
              the CAD drawing when manufacturing the tray handles
              using the CAM router.
              Answers describing the reason for the red lines should be awarded up to
              3 marks.

              No answer or no relevant information presented or discussed.                     0
              Simplistic or basic response

              The red lines show where the tray needs to be cut
              A more detailed explanation or response
              The red lines show where the tray sides need to be cut for the tool path         2
              for the CNC router
              A detailed explanation or response
              The red lines are contour lines which show where the centre of the CNC
              toolpath will be, allowing the black outline (tray side) to be cut accurately.
              If there were no red lines, the black lines used as a tool path would            3
              produce a smaller tray side (dependent on the diameter of the cutting
              tool)
